title: Same trip, same loss: why was one insurance claim rejected?
summary: In situations where two insured individuals are affected by the same insured event but receive contradictory decisions, it is reasonable to seek clarification based on such differential treatment. An insurer should be able to explain why identical facts have resulted in different outcomes.

title: Bengaluru landowner sold 17 flats, made ₹11.8 crore and paid no tax — then won the tax dispute | Know how
summary: A Bengaluru resident won a tax dispute over capital gains of ₹11.8 crore from selling 17 apartments. Although the income tax department initially rejected his tax exemption claims, the ITAT ruled he could claim exemptions on multiple properties, as each sale was an independent transaction.

title: India's CPI inflation is 4.38%, but your personal inflation could be higher. Here's how to calculate it

summary: With two crore income tax returns already filed, experts advise taxpayers to complete their ITR filing before the 31 July deadline. Early submission can help avoid last-minute errors, resolve tax mismatches, claim refunds faster and ensure smooth compliance without depending on an extension.

title: IIAS opposes Ujjivan SFB RSU plan over disclosure concerns
summary: Proxy advisory firm IIAS opposed Ujjivan Small Finance Bank’s proposed RSU scheme, citing inadequate clarity on vesting conditions and performance metrics. The bank responded that vesting depends on exceeding revenue and profit targets and satisfactory individual performance ratings.

title: RBI proposes easing rules for mutual funds, insurers to acquire higher stake in banks
summary: The RBI has proposed one-time approval for mutual funds, insurers and pension funds to increase bank shareholding up to 10% without repeated regulatory clearance. The move aims to simplify compliance for significant institutional investors, with public comments invited until August 4, 2026.

title: Rs 1,839 crore Biocon block deal: ICICI Pru MF biggest buyer along with Citi and Goldman Sachs
summary: Mylan sold a ₹1,839 crore stake in Biocon through a block deal, attracting strong institutional demand. ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund emerged as the largest buyer, alongside other mutual funds, insurers and foreign investors, highlighting continued confidence in the pharmaceutical company.
